title: Revolve Q4 2024 Earnings Transcript Summary
text: Revolve exceeded expectations in Q4 2024 with strong results across key metrics. Revenue and Profitability: Net sales reached $294 million, up 14% year-over-year, surpassing the $282.67 million forecast. Net income tripled to $12 million ($0.17 per diluted share), beating the $0.10 EPS estimate by 70%. Adjusted EBITDA jumped 114% to $18 million year-over-year. Segment Performance: Revolve segment net sales increased 15%. Forward segment net sales increased 11%. International sales grew 14% despite being a traditionally challenging market. Strategic Achievements: The company highlighted progress on multiple growth initiatives with a nearly 130 basis point reduction in variable logistics costs, driven by operational efficiencies and improved return rates. Key growth drivers included expansion into beauty, men's, and home product categories, AI-powered merchandising and search optimization, own brand development reaching 18% of Revolve segment sales, and physical retail testing through pop-up shops and permanent locations. Despite beating expectations, the stock declined 4.74% in after-hours trading to $28.25, reflecting broader market concerns. For 2025, management guided gross margin of 52.4-52.9% and marketing spend of 14.9-15.1% of net sales, targeting continued profitable growth while investing in emerging opportunities.
